summary: Partially decompiled the staff-roll initializer `gm_801AC6D8` in `src/melee/gm/gm_1A36.c`, moving it from an asm placeholder to a large C implementation that initializes effects, SIS text data, audio, archives, cameras, lights, fog, models, GObj callbacks, and related staff-roll globals. The PR also renamed scene-minor helpers in symbols/asm to `gm_SetSceneMinor` and `gm_SetScenePendingMinor`, added many typed prototypes and local/static data declarations, and adjusted support headers for archive, SIS, audio, light, and background-flash calls. The decomp.dev bot reported `gm_801AC6D8` improving from 0.00% to 74.12% (+1085 bytes), with some small matching regressions elsewhere.
what_changed: Replaced the `/// #gm_801AC6D8` placeholder with a large C implementation for the staff-roll setup path.

`gm_801AC6D8` now allocates staff-roll state buffers with `HSD_MemAlloc`, initializes effects via `efLib_8005B4B8` and `efAsync_8006737C`, initializes SIS data with `HSD_SisLib_803A62A0`/`HSD_SisLib_803A611C`, starts audio setup, loads `GmStRoll.dat`, and creates multiple GObjs for cameras, lights, fog, models, and staff-roll name/model data.

Added typed staff-roll static structs in `gm_1A36.static.h`, including placeholder-sized structs with `STATIC_ASSERT` for `gm_804D67F8_t`, `gm_804D67FC_t`, `gm_80480D58_t`, and `gm_803DBFD8_t`, plus typed archive scene-data structures for cameras, fogs, lights, and models.

Added many static/global declarations for staff-roll-related data such as `gm_804D67F8`, `gm_804D67FC`, `gm_804D6804`, `gm_804D682C`, `gm_804D6830`, `gm_804D6834`, `gm_804D6838`, `gm_804D6840`, `gm_804D6844`, `gm_804D6848`, and `gm_804D684C`.

Renamed symbol-table entries and asm callsites from `gm_801A428C` to `gm_SetSceneMinor` and from `gm_801A42A0` to `gm_SetScenePendingMinor`; the implementations set `curr_minor`/`prev_minor` and `pending_minor = pending_minor + 1` respectively.

Updated numerous asm callsite labels in `gm_1A36.s`, `gm_1601.s`, and `un_2FC9.s` to use clearer callee names such as `memzero`, `lbArchive_LoadSymbols`, debug printer names, screenshot helpers, and the new scene-minor names.

Corrected or specialized support prototypes: `lb_80011AC4(HSD_LightDesc**)`, `lbAudioAx_80026F2C` returning `u64`, `lbAudioAx_80027648(void)`, `lbBgFlash_800209F4(void)`, `HSD_SisLib_803A945C(char*)` returning `HSD_Archive*`, and `HSD_SisLib_803A62A0(s32, char*, char*)`.
Changed `HSD_SisLib_803A62A0` to accept an archive filename string, call `HSD_SisLib_803A945C(arg1)`, and store the returned archive pointer rather than taking an `HSD_Archive*` directly.
Renamed the `vi0102_data` light field from an untyped `void* lightdata` to `HSD_LightDesc** lights`, matching the updated `lb_80011AC4` signature.
Added `-Wno-for-loop-analysis` to `compile_flags.txt`, likely to tolerate an unusual matching-oriented loop pattern in this slice.
smart_moves: Used approximate-but-size-locked placeholder structs with `STATIC_ASSERT` to give the compiler enough type and size information without requiring the full structure layout.

Typed the archive scene-data access enough to express `gm_804D6840->cameras`, `->lights`, `->fogs`, and `->models`, which made the initializer readable and enabled direct C calls into HSD object loading APIs.
Grouped the C initializer into scoped blocks per created GObj, limiting temporary variable lifetimes and matching repeated object-construction patterns for camera, light, fog, and model setup.
Converted magic address-named helpers to semantic names for scene-minor state transitions, making hundreds of asm callsites more searchable and making future game-mode decomp easier.
Improved support-library prototypes in adjacent headers instead of forcing casts at the callsite; this carries useful type information forward for later matches.
Preserved TODO annotations where evidence was incomplete, such as the possible `SceneDesc` type for the archive pointer and the noted length of `gm_804D6844`.
decomp_lessons: For large initializer functions, partial matching can be unlocked by first reconstructing resource/archive descriptor shapes and global state types, even if many internal structs are still opaque.

If a function is mostly object creation and setup, matching-friendly C can mirror the assembly by keeping each GObj setup sequence in a small lexical block with local `gobj`, `cobj`, `jobj`, `lobj`, or `fog` temporaries.
Semantic renames such as `gm_SetSceneMinor` and `gm_SetScenePendingMinor` are high leverage because they clarify behavior across both C and still-asm files.
Use `STATIC_ASSERT` whenever a placeholder struct represents an allocation size or fixed data object; this prevents silent drift while allowing incremental struct recovery.
Correcting callee prototypes in library headers can be necessary for an unrelated game-mode match because return type and argument width affect register use.
When source evidence is weak for a type, keep the type provisional and mark it with a TODO rather than overclaiming a final structure name.
Bot match reports should be interpreted function-by-function: this PR significantly improved `gm_801AC6D8` but also broke or regressed a few nearby matches.
naming_conventions: `gm_801A428C` was promoted to semantic name `gm_SetSceneMinor` in symbols and asm labels.
`gm_801A42A0` was promoted to semantic name `gm_SetScenePendingMinor` in symbols and asm labels.
Scene-minor helper parameter naming uses behavior-oriented names such as `pending_minor` rather than generic `arg0` where meaning is known.
Placeholder static structs for anonymous globals use the pattern `struct gm_<address>_t`, for example `gm_804D67F8_t`, `gm_804D67FC_t`, and `gm_80480D58_t`.
Typed nested archive descriptor structs use global-derived prefixes such as `gm_804D6840_cameras_t`, `gm_804D6840_fogs_t`, and `gm_804D6840_t`.
Fields with unknown semantics remain mechanically named by offset, such as `x0`, `x4`, `x8`, and `pad_0`, while still preserving size and offset information.

Support-library function names reused existing project semantic names where known, such as `memzero`, `lbArchive_LoadSymbols`, `db_PrintEntityCounts`, `db_PrintThreadInfo`, `db_CheckScreenshot`, and `db_TakeScreenshotIfPending`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Removed part of `asm/melee/gm/gm_1A36.s` by adding a C body for `gm_801AC6D8`, producing a reported +1085 byte improvement for that function.
Kept many still-asm callsites synchronized with symbol renames so references to scene-minor setters resolve to the semantic labels.
Used exact-size placeholder structures and `sizeof(struct ...)` in allocations and `memzero` calls to preserve constants such as `0x948`, `0x2E68`, and `0x18`.
Used explicit constants for GObj class/priorities and GX links, including a local `int const gx_link = 11`, to express repeated setup values while preserving assembly-like behavior.
Used `M2C_FIELD` for unresolved deep pointer-field manipulation in the language-specific JObj case instead of inventing a final nested struct layout.

The unusual child-reparent loop in the staff-roll model setup appears to rely on side effects of `HSD_JObjReparent`, and the PR added `-Wno-for-loop-analysis` to permit this matching-oriented construct.
Some improvements came from callee-signature fixes, for example changing `lbAudioAx_80026F2C` to return `u64` for use as the second argument to `lbAudioAx_8002702C`.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the slice.
The decomp.dev bot reported matched code at 27.17%, down 0.01% overall (-320 bytes).
The bot reported `gm_801AC6D8` improved from 0.00% to 74.12% (+1085 bytes) and `fn_801A396C` improved from 21.21% to 27.27% (+16 bytes).
The bot also reported broken matches in `gm_801A3714` and `gm_801A36E0`, plus regressions in `gm_801A3A74`, `HSD_SisLib_803A62A0`, and `gm_801A3820`.
follow_up_queries: Check later PRs for full matching or cleanup of `gm_801AC6D8` after the 74.12% partial match.
Search for later replacement of `gm_804D6840_t` TODO with a shared `SceneDesc` or staff-roll scene descriptor type.
Look for follow-up fixes to the bot-reported regressions in `gm_801A3714`, `gm_801A36E0`, `gm_801A3A74`, and `HSD_SisLib_803A62A0`.
Search whether `gm_SetSceneMinor` and `gm_SetScenePendingMinor` were later reflected consistently in public headers as well as symbols/asm.
Investigate later PRs around `HSD_SisLib_803A62A0` because this PR changed its API and bot-reported a match regression there.
